title: Haakaa Silicone Breast Pump
summary: A simple one-piece silicone pump that attaches via suction and collects letdown milk hands-free while you nurse on the other side. An effortless way to build a freezer stash.
body:
What the Haakaa Is (and Isn't)

The Haakaa is a single-piece silicone pump — no tubing, no motor, no fiddly parts. You squeeze the bulb, place it over your breast, and release. The suction holds it in place and collects milk that flows due to the gentle negative pressure and, more importantly, your natural letdown reflex.

It's not a pump in the active sense that an electric pump is. It doesn't extract milk the way a Spectra or Medela does. What it does is collect the milk that would otherwise soak into a nursing pad on the side you're not actively feeding on — the "letdown leak" that happens simultaneously on both breasts during a feed.

That's a meaningful distinction. The Haakaa is a collection device first, a passive pump second. Many parents use it alongside an electric pump rather than instead of one.

The Freezer Stash Argument

The most compelling use case is building a freezer stash without any extra pumping sessions. If you're nursing on the left side, attach the Haakaa to the right, collect 30–60ml per feed, and freeze it. Over days and weeks, that adds up to a meaningful reserve — all without sitting down to pump separately.

For parents who want a freezer stash but dread adding pumping sessions to an already demanding newborn schedule, the Haakaa is a remarkably low-effort solution.

Practical Considerations

A few things to know before you buy:

Suction strength matters. The Haakaa's collection depends on how well it seals to your breast. Some parents find it stays perfectly in place; others find it falls off or doesn't collect consistently. Body shape affects this.

It can be too stimulating for some. For parents prone to oversupply, using the Haakaa on the opposite breast at every feed may exacerbate the issue. If you're already producing more than baby needs, use it selectively.

It's not a replacement for an electric pump if you need to maintain supply while away from baby. For that use case, a single or double electric pump that actively extracts milk is necessary.

It's extremely easy to clean. One piece, no corners or valves — rinse, boil or sterilise, done. This simplicity is a genuine advantage in the newborn phase.

Price Point

The Haakaa retails for a fraction of the cost of an electric pump — often under £20 / $25. For what it does, it's one of the best-value breastfeeding purchases available. Many parents buy it as an automatic add-on alongside their primary pump.

The 150ml Version vs. the 100ml Version

Haakaa comes in two sizes. The 100ml version is more compact but fills up faster; the 150ml is the better choice if you're a higher producer or want fewer interruptions to empty it. Both work the same way — it's purely a capacity question.

Bottom Line

The Haakaa is not magic, but for what it costs and how little effort it requires, it's one of the easiest breastfeeding tools to recommend. If you're planning to breastfeed and want a freezer stash without adding pump sessions to your day, buy one. You might be surprised how quickly it pays for itself.
